content:
# Batched _process_batch — Design Decision (2026-04-10)

## What
Refactor `_process_batch()` in raune_filter.py to do single batched H2D upload,
batched downscale, batched RAUNE+OKLab, single batched D2H. Update Triton wrapper
to accept (N, 3, H, W) by flattening N into the pixel dimension.

## Why
Performance reviewers (PR #65, #67) flagged that per-frame upload loops dominate
when GPU compute is no longer the bottleneck. fp16 cut GPU compute from 398→230ms,
exposing the per-frame overhead. batch=8 only gives +0.7%, batch=16 regresses.

## Key insight
The Triton OKLab kernel processes pixels independently — flattening N into
`n_pixels` is mathematically valid. Wrapper-only change, kernel itself untouched.

## Approach
- np.stack frames CPU-side, single .cuda() call
- Reuse full_batch tensor for both downscale (proxy) and transfer (full-res)
- Batched F.interpolate for both downscale and delta upscale
- Wrapper permutes (N,3,H,W)→(3,N,H,W) for channel-first contiguous layout

## Cost
Permute+contiguous in wrapper: ~0.6ms at batch=8 4K fp16. Negligible vs the
~N × ~25ms PCIe uploads it eliminates.

## Expected
- batch=4: unchanged ~4.25 fps
- batch=8: ~5 fps (target)
- batch=16: ~5-6 fps (no longer regresses)

## Issue
chunzhe10/dorea#68

## Spec
docs/decisions/2026-04-10-batched-process-batch.md
